questions.question: Identify the vocabulary word for 'a person who speaks two or more languages'
questions.explanation: A bilingual person is someone who speaks two languages.

questions.question: Choose the correct vocabulary word for 'a person who is an expert in language'
questions.explanation: A linguist is a person who studies language.

questions.question: Identify the vocabulary word for 'words that have the same spelling but different meanings'
questions.explanation: Homographs are words that have the same spelling but different meanings.

questions.question: Choose the correct vocabulary word for 'words that have the same pronunciation but different meanings'
questions.explanation: Homophones are words that have the same pronunciation but different meanings.

questions.question: Identify the vocabulary word for 'a word that has the opposite meaning of another word'
questions.explanation: An antonym is a word that has the opposite meaning of another word.

questions.question: Choose the correct prepositional phrase to complete the sentence 'The new policy will be implemented _______ the beginning of the next fiscal year'.
questions.explanation: The preposition 'from' indicates the starting point of an action or period.

questions.question: Choose the correct compound word for the phrase 'a room in a house for cooking'.
questions.explanation: A kitchen is a room in a house for cooking.

questions.question: Identify the correct cliché for the phrase 'a difficult or unpleasant task'.
questions.explanation: A tough nut to crack is a cliché for a difficult or unpleasant task.

questions.question: Choose the correct American English variation of the word 'cheque'.
questions.explanation: In American English, 'cheque' is spelled as 'check'.

questions.question: Identify the correct form of inversion in the sentence 'Not only did the teacher explain the lesson, but also she gave notes.'
questions.explanation: Inversion occurs when the sentence structure is reversed for emphasis, here it's partial inversion.

questions.question: Identify the correct form of inversion in the sentence 'Not only did the teacher explain the lesson but also gave notes'
options: Not only did the teacher explain
Did the teacher not only explain
The teacher did not only explain
questions.explanation: Inversion occurs when the negative adverbial phrase begins the sentence.

questions.question: Choose the correct prepositional phrase to fill the blank in 'The new policy is _______ effect from January'
questions.explanation: The correct prepositional phrase is 'in effect' which means something is officially working or being used.

questions.question: Change the sentence 'She said, “I will meet you tomorrow.”' into reported speech.
options: She said that she would meet me tomorrow.
She said that she will meet me tomorrow.
She said that I will meet her tomorrow.
She said that she can meet me tomorrow.
questions.explanation: When changing direct speech to reported speech, the pronouns and verb tenses are adjusted to fit the new context. The correct reported speech sentence is 'She said that she would meet me tomorrow' because it correctly changes the verb tense and pronoun.

questions.question: Identify the correct form of reported speech for the sentence 'He said, “My mother is ill.”'
options: He said that his mother was ill.
He said that his mother is ill.
He said that my mother is ill.
He said that my mother was ill.
questions.explanation: When changing direct speech to reported speech, the pronouns and verb tenses are adjusted to fit the new context. The correct reported speech sentence is 'He said that his mother was ill' because it correctly changes the verb tense and pronoun.

questions.question: Change the sentence 'The teacher said, “Do not make noise in the classroom.”' into reported speech.
options: The teacher said that we should not make noise in the classroom.
The teacher said that we must not make noise in the classroom.
The teacher said that we should not have made noise in the classroom.
The teacher said that we must not have made noise in the classroom.
questions.explanation: When changing direct speech to reported speech, the pronouns and verb tenses are adjusted to fit the new context. The correct reported speech sentence is 'The teacher said that we must not make noise in the classroom' because it correctly changes the verb tense and pronoun.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'chips' (British English) and 'fries' (American English)?
options: Chips are thicker, fries are thinner
Chips are thinner, fries are thicker
Chips are used for cooking, fries are used for serving
Chips are used in Britain, fries are used in America
questions.explanation: British English and American English have different vocabulary for some words, including 'chips' and 'fries'. The correct difference is that 'chips' are used in Britain, while 'fries' are used in America, both referring to thinly sliced and fried potatoes.
